recent_coach_change: No change; Scaloni continues since 2018 and won the 2022 World Cup and 2024 Copa America.
qualification_path: Qualified via CONMEBOL South American qualifying, finishing top of the table well ahead of the field as the first nation to secure a 2026 berth.
key_players: Lionel Messi (Inter Miami) — captain and talisman, returning from late-May muscle injury
Lautaro Martinez (Inter Milan) — main striker, scored from the spot vs Honduras warm-up
Julian Alvarez (Atletico Madrid) — versatile forward, strong club season
Emiliano Martinez (Aston Villa) — first-choice goalkeeper recovering from a finger fracture
Cristian Romero (Tottenham) — defensive leader managing a knee ligament issue

injuries_doubts.issue: muscle fatigue / left hamstring strain (sustained 25 May with Inter Miami)
fractured ring finger from the Europa League final
sprained collateral ligament in right knee
injury ruling him out of the squad

squad_notes: Squad built on continuity: 17 of the 26 are 2022 Qatar champions. Paulo Dybala omitted (no call-up since September 2024). Giovani Lo Celso returns after missing 2022 through injury. Scaloni delayed the final 26 to the last minute to assess fitness of several core players.
warmup_assessment: Solid. Beat Honduras 2-0 on 6 June (Lautaro Martinez penalty, Giuliano Simeone) with Messi rested; a second friendly vs Iceland follows on 9 June. Scaloni rotated and avoided risking fitness-doubt players.
shape_verdict: Defending champions look in good collective shape with depth and continuity, though several key starters including Messi, Emi Martinez and Romero are managing knocks heading into the opener.

news.headline: Argentina beat Honduras 2-0 in pre-World Cup friendly as Messi sits out
Scaloni calms Messi injury fears, confirms captain in World Cup squad
Paulo Dybala excluded from Argentina's preliminary World Cup roster
